text.title: Journey Mapping Output Contract
text.summary: JSON Schema (draft-07) that pins the shape of a valid journey-map artefact. Subagents validate against this before returning.

example.code: { "artefact_id": "journey-shop-purchase-2026-05-23", "owner": "Maria Yuskiv <maria@faion.net>", "version": "1.0.0", "last_reviewed": "2026-05-23", "map_type": "current_state", "persona": "Sarah, mid-market buyer", "scope": {"start": "first ad impression", "end": "first product use"}, "stages": [ { "name": "Discover", "actions": [{"text": "Sees ad on Instagram", "evidence": "INT-04"}], "touchpoints": [{"text": "Instagram", "evidence": "ANL-funnel-2026-05"}], "thoughts": [{"text": "Is this for me?", "evidence": "INT-04"}], "emotions": {"score": 3, "label": "curious", "evidence": "INT-04"}, "pain_points": [{"text": "no data", "evidence": "no data"}], "opportunities": [{"text": "Tighten ad targeting", "linked_backlog_id": "BL-211"}] } ] }

example.note: Owner plural; map_type not in enum; scope missing end; stages missing required components; below minItems.

forbidden.pattern: Map cell with prose but no `evidence` field and not literal "no data".
Stage names from CRM pipeline ("Lead", "Opportunity", "Closed") instead of user perspective.
`map_type == "future_state"` without explicit "Design Hypothesis" disclaimer in artefact header or scope.
Multiple personas combined in one map.
